questions: The `image_generation` resolver requires `model_name` to be 'qwen-image' or 'z-image' for reference params to work (style/subject/scene). Should the agent default to 'qwen-image' for reference tasks, or should the user/prompt control model selection?

The idea mentions image-to-video tasks. There's no simple i2v resolver in create-task — video generation uses complex orchestration (travel_between_images, join_clips, edit_video_orchestrator). Should we scope i2v out of this initial implementation and focus on image generation with reference modes?

Should shot creation be automatic (agent always creates a shot for task results) or explicit (agent decides based on user intent)? The idea says 'create a new shot containing the selected anchor images' but it's unclear if this should happen for every task.

The existing `generate` command appears to use the old `task_type`/`params` API format which the create-task edge function no longer accepts. Is this already known to be broken, or is there a fallback I'm missing?

success_criteria.criterion: Agent can create style-transfer tasks using selected clip URLs as reference images
Agent can create subject-transfer tasks using selected clip URLs
Agent can create scene-transfer tasks using selected clip URLs
Basic text-to-image generation (no reference) still works via the agent
Task creation uses the family/input API format expected by create-task edge function
Agent can create a new shot and associate generated results with it
System prompt clearly explains when and how to use each task type
create_task tool has structured JSON parameters (not text parsing) for reliable LLM usage
Selected clip URLs from the frontend are correctly passed through to the reference image parameters
Existing timeline editing commands (move, trim, delete, etc.) are unaffected
New tool integrates cleanly with the existing agent loop (tool_call/tool_result turn format, error handling, auto-continuation)
Supabase edge function deployed and tested with real task creation

assumptions: The create-task edge function now requires `family`/`input` format — the old `task_type`/`params` format the agent uses is broken or deprecated. Will fix as part of this work.
Style/subject/scene transfer are all handled by the `image_generation` family with `reference_mode` parameter — no new resolvers needed.
Image-to-video is out of scope for this initial implementation due to complexity of video orchestration resolvers.
The agent will default to `qwen-image` model when reference images are used, since only qwen-image and z-image support reference params in the resolver.
Shot creation will be optional/explicit — the agent creates a shot only when the user asks or when it makes contextual sense, not automatically for every task.
The `create_task` tool will be a separate structured tool alongside `run`, not a new text command, for reliability with complex parameters.
The agent already receives selected clip URLs via the `selected_clips` payload — no frontend changes needed.
